user_story: As an estimator, I want to upload a client BOM (PDF or XLSX) and receive a draft quotation, so that I don't price every line by hand.
expected_behaviour: Accepts multipart form (file + tier + customer), runs parse->extract->normalize->match->price->quote and returns {quotation, matched}. Missing file returns 400 with a clear message. Empty/garbled file yields zero lines, not a crash. The pipeline takes ~30-60s so the contract must let the caller show a designed loading state (ux:states-are-features).
test_method: POST /api/quote multipart bom.xlsx + missing-file edge case
test_result: pass: 15/16 matched returned; missing file -> 400

user_story: As the system, I want to extract text from a PDF BOM so a contractor's emailed PDF can be quoted.
expected_behaviour: new LiteParse({outputFormat:'markdown'}).parse(path) -> result.text. Text-based PDFs extract cleanly; scanned PDFs route through bundled Tesseract OCR. Returns markdown for the extractor.
test_result: pass: liteparse text -> 15/16 matched

user_story: As the system, I want to read a spreadsheet BOM as a cell grid so an Excel BOM can be quoted.
expected_behaviour: read() first sheet -> sheet_to_json header:1 -> string grid, rendered as TSV for the extractor. .xlsx/.xls/.csv route here; everything else routes to PDF.
test_result: pass: grid -> 15/16 matched

user_story: As an estimator, I want only the real supply lines pulled out of a messy BOM, so headers/totals/notes don't become quote lines.
expected_behaviour: LLM returns {description, qty, unit, code} per orderable line; drops headers, section titles, column headers, subtotals, totals, and free-text notes. Empty content -> [] (no crash).
test_method: pipeline run on 16-line sample BOM with header+notes rows
test_result: pass: 16 orderable lines extracted, header/notes/totals dropped

user_story: As an estimator, I want trade shorthand expanded and specs parsed, so the matcher and I read the same canonical line.
expected_behaviour: Expands Cu/PVC/SP/ELCB/SPN/MCB/DB etc.; parses cores/csa/rating/poles/curve/kA/size/watt/ways; canonicalises units to nos/m/set/lot; coil->100m via unitFactor; qty defaults to 1.
test_method: pipeline run; inspected normalized text + specs in summary
test_result: pass: '3C x 2.5 sqmm Cu/PVC'->canonical; coil->100m; specs parsed

user_story: As an estimator, I want each line resolved to a catalog part with a confidence, so I trust auto-matches and review the rest.
expected_behaviour: samesake entity resolution: cosine over the description embedding + pg_trgm over the code/alias searchkey -> ranked candidates with combined confidence; candidate mapped back to the catalog part by description.
test_method: pipeline run on sample BOM (real gemini + samesake + Postgres)
test_result: pass: 15/16 lines matched, off-catalog item unmatched

user_story: As an estimator, I want a 32A line to never silently match a 40A part, so a wrong-variant quote is impossible.
expected_behaviour: Hard specs (ratingA, poles, csaMm2, cores, sizeMm, watt, ways) must not conflict (poles canonicalised SP/DP/TP/TPN/4P). top>=autoLink AND compatible -> matched; >=suggest -> review; else unmatched. If specs eliminate all, surface raw for review.
test_method: sample BOM: 18W vs 24W panel, 32A SP/DP/TP breakers
test_result: pass: 18W picked over 24W (watt gate); breakers matched after poles canonicalisation

user_story: As an estimator, I want every price to come from the company rules with a visible trace, so quotes are auditable and consistent.
expected_behaviour: Per line: list * (1 + categoryMarkup + brandMargin) * (1 - tierDiscount - best qtyBreak), rounded to priceDecimals; priceTrace records each rule. Reads only data/pricing-rules.json (lift-and-shift).
test_result: pass: list 410 -> unit 321.44 (-2% brand, -18% tier, -2% qty>=200), trace present

user_story: As an estimator, I want subtotal, savings, taxes and grand total computed, plus unresolved lines flagged, so I know what's final.
expected_behaviour: Prices matched lines; subtotal, discountTotal, each tax on subtotal, grandTotal; validUntil = today+validityDays; unresolved surfaced separately; notes warn about review count + lead time.
test_result: pass: VAT 18% = 43,471.71, grand LKR 284,981.21, 1 unresolved flagged

user_story: As an estimator, I want a branded PDF I can send the client, so the quote looks professional.
expected_behaviour: pdf-lib A4: letterhead, quote no/date/validity, customer+tier, line table with per-line price trace, totals block, notes, footer. WinAnsi-safe. Paginates on overflow.
test_result: pass: valid PDF-1.7; professional layout, letterhead+table+traces+totals legible

feature: Manual catalog search for a line
user_story: As an estimator reviewing a low-confidence line, I want to search the catalog by text, so I can pick the right part myself.
expected_behaviour: POST {text, limit?} -> {candidates: MatchCandidate[]} via samesake match. Recognition over recall: ranked parts to choose from (ux:cut-the-load).
test_result: pass: candidates returned (top ABB/SCH 32A SP)

user_story: As an estimator, I want my override to improve future matches, so the system learns our catalog vocabulary.
expected_behaviour: POST {queryText, chosenCode} resolves the code to its entity id and calls matcher.confirm; null/unknown -> no-op success. Feedback persists so similar text ranks the confirmed part higher.
test_result: fail: 500 Internal Server Error
error_detail: Passed the catalog code as chosenEntityId, but samesake confirm does BigInt(chosenEntityId) (packages/server/src/core/match.ts:402) -> throws on a non-numeric code -> 500. The decline branch passed declinedEntityId:'' -> same crash.
fix_summary: Added a code->entity_id index (buildCodeIndex queries entity_part external_id/id after setup); /api/confirm now resolves chosenCode->entity id then confirms, and returns a no-op success for null/unknown codes (dropped the broken decline branch).

user_story: As an estimator, I want to download the PDF after fixing matches, so the final quote reflects my overrides.
expected_behaviour: POST {matched, tier, customer} -> re-assembles the quotation and streams application/pdf with a Content-Disposition filename. Reflects the (possibly overridden) matched array.
test_result: pass: application/pdf returned, %PDF header, Content-Disposition set

user_story: As an estimator, I want totals to update when I change a line, so I see the price impact immediately (reward now).
expected_behaviour: POST {line, tier, customer} -> {quoteLine} for a matched line (null if no chosen). Lets the UI live-update without re-running the pipeline.
test_result: pass: quoteLine.lineTotal = 80360

user_story: As the UI, I want the company, rules (tiers), and catalog, so I can render the header, the tier picker, and the override search.
expected_behaviour: GET /api/config -> {company, rules}; GET /api/catalog -> CatalogPart[]. Drive the tier select labels and currency from config (recognition over recall).
test_result: pass: Voltline + 4 tiers; 43 catalog parts

user_story: As an operator, I want to embed the catalog into samesake (and reset after edits), so matching works against current parts.
expected_behaviour: bun run setup applies the entity schema + upserts/embeds every catalog part (idempotent). --reset drops project_<slug> first. Fails fast with a clear message if DATABASE_URL or GEMINI_API_KEY is missing.
test_result: pass: dropped + recreated schema, 43 parts embedded

user_story: As an operator, I want to quote a BOM file from the terminal, so I can test without the UI.
expected_behaviour: bun run quote [file] -> console match summary (status per line) + data/quote-output.{pdf,json}. Defaults to the sample BOM. Requires DATABASE_URL + GEMINI_API_KEY.
test_method: bun run quote (xlsx) and bun run quote bom.pdf
test_result: pass: per-line summary + valid pdf/json written

user_story: As an operator, I want a realistic messy sample BOM (xlsx + pdf), so I can demo and test the pipeline.
expected_behaviour: Writes data/sample-bom/bom.{xlsx,pdf} with 16 lines of trade shorthand incl. one off-catalog item, exercising extraction, normalization, spec-gating, and the review path.
test_result: pass: bom.xlsx (18KB) + bom.pdf (3KB), 16 lines

user_story: As an estimator, I want to drop in a BOM file and set the customer and pricing tier, so I can start a quote in seconds.
expected_behaviour: Drag/drop or click-to-browse, accepts .xlsx/.pdf only; shows the chosen filename; customer text input; tier select populated from /api/config with sensible default; Generate disabled until a file + config are ready (ux:cut-the-load, recognition over recall).
test_method: vite build + tsc + code review (dropzone, accept filter, tier from config)
test_result: pass: builds, 0 tsc errors, tier select bound to config; no browser click-through

user_story: As an estimator, I want clear feedback while the quote computes, so a 30-60s wait doesn't look frozen (ux:states-are-features).
expected_behaviour: On Generate: POST /api/quote (multipart), show a spinner cycling stage labels (Parsing/Extracting/Matching/Pricing), disable the button, surface errors in an error banner, clear prior results.
test_result: pass: designed loading + error states present (stage labels are time-based, not real progress — acceptable)

user_story: As an estimator, I want each line's match, confidence, and price in one table, so I can scan what's trustworthy vs needs attention.
expected_behaviour: One row per matched line: status pill (matched/review/unmatched), qty+unit, normalized description, matched part (code+brand) with a confidence bar+%, line total. Empty/no-match rows render 'No match'.
test_result: pass: pills, confidence bar, totals rendered; unmatched shows 'No match'

user_story: As an estimator, I want to correct a flagged line from suggestions or a catalog search, so I never ship a wrong part.
expected_behaviour: review/unmatched rows show an Override select of alternatives + a Search catalog box (POST /api/match); choosing one sets chosen, flips to matched, fires /api/confirm to teach the matcher, and re-prices via /api/price (ux:recognition-over-recall, reward-now).
test_result: pass: override + search + confirm + reprice wired; override surfaced only on flagged rows (matched rows are spec-gated reliable — enhancement, not defect)

user_story: As an estimator, I want subtotal, savings, taxes and grand total that update as I fix lines, so I see the price as I work (ux:reward-now).
expected_behaviour: Summary recomputes from the priced lines client-side: subtotal, total savings (if any), each tax with %, grand total in the company currency; updates when an override re-prices a line.
test_result: pass: totals derived from priced lines, currency from config

user_story: As an estimator, I want to download the final branded PDF, so I can send it to the client (ux:design-the-end).
expected_behaviour: Download button POSTs the (overridden) matched lines to /api/quote/pdf and triggers a file download named after the quote no; disabled while generating or when no priced lines.
test_result: pass: blob download wired to the verified PDF endpoint
